question: What dua should a Muslim say before eating?
explanation: A Muslim should say 'Bismillah' (In the name of Allah) before eating. The Prophet (SAW) instructed: 'When one of you eats, let him mention the name of Allah.' If one forgets at the start, they should say 'Bismillahi fi awwalihi wa akhirihi' (In the name of Allah at its beginning and its end).

source.name: Sunan Abu Dawud; Jami at-Tirmidhi
source.reference: Sunan Abu Dawud Book 27, Hadith 3767; Jami at-Tirmidhi Book 25, Hadith 1858

question: What do Muslims say when someone sneezes?

options.A: The sneezing person says 'Alhamdulillah' and those who hear respond 'Yarhamukallah'
options.C: The sneezing person says 'Bismillah' and others say 'Alhamdulillah'

explanation: When a Muslim sneezes, they say 'Alhamdulillah' (All praise is due to Allah). Those who hear respond with 'Yarhamukallah' (May Allah have mercy on you). The sneezing person then replies 'Yahdikumullahu wa yuslihu balakum' (May Allah guide you and rectify your affairs). This exchange is an established sunnah.

question: What dua is recommended when entering the home?

options.B: Bismillahi walajna wa bismillahi kharajna wa ala Rabbina tawakkalna

explanation: The recommended dua when entering the home is: 'Bismillahi walajna wa bismillahi kharajna wa ala Rabbina tawakkalna' — meaning 'In the name of Allah we enter, in the name of Allah we leave, and upon our Lord we place our trust.' The Prophet (SAW) taught this dua and said that Shaytan is repelled when it is recited.

question: What should a Muslim say when they hear about the death of someone?

options.B: La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
options.C: Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un

explanation: 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un' means 'Indeed, to Allah we belong and to Him we shall return.' This statement of patience and submission is commanded in the Quran upon being struck by calamity. Allah promises mercy and guidance to those who recite it during times of loss.

question: What dua do Muslims say after completing the wudu (ablution)?

options.B: Ashhadu an la ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lah, wa ashhadu anna Muhammadan abduhu wa rasuluh

explanation: After completing wudu, the Prophet (SAW) taught the dua of the Shahada: 'Ashhadu an la ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lah, wa ashhadu anna Muhammadan abduhu wa rasuluh.' He said that whoever recites this after wudu will have the eight gates of Paradise opened for them to enter through whichever they wish.

question: What is the dua a Muslim should say when beginning any important matter or action?
explanation: 'Bismillah ir-Rahman ir-Rahim' (In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful) is recommended before beginning any important action or matter. It is the opening verse of the Quran and 113 of the 114 Surahs. The Prophet (SAW) said any important matter not begun with Bismillah is cut off from blessing.

source.name: Sunan Ibn Majah; Quran Surah Al-Fatiha
source.reference: Sunan Ibn Majah Book 1, Hadith 1; Surah Al-Fatiha 1:1

question: What is the dua said when entering the masjid?

options.D: Allahumma barik lana fi hadhal masjid

explanation: When entering the masjid, the recommended dua is 'Allahummaftah li abwaba rahmatik' — meaning 'O Allah, open for me the gates of Your mercy.' This is recited after sending salawat (blessings) upon the Prophet (SAW). When leaving, one says 'Allahumma inni as'aluka min fadlik' (O Allah, I ask of You from Your bounty).

question: Which dua did Prophet Yunus (AS) recite while in the belly of the whale, as mentioned in the Quran?

options.A: Rabbi inni massaniya addurru wa anta arhamur rahimin
options.C: La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu minaz zalimin
options.D: Rabbi inni lima anzalta ilayya min khayrin faqir

explanation: Prophet Yunus (AS) called out from the darkness of the whale's belly: 'La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu minaz zalimin' — meaning 'There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers.' Allah responded to this dua and saved him, and the Quran states that this dua will similarly save any believer who calls with it.

question: What is the dua known as Sayyid al-Istighfar (the Master of Seeking Forgiveness), and what did the Prophet (SAW) say about it?

options.B: Allahumma anta rabbi la ilaha illa anta khalaqtani wa ana abduka...
options.C: Astaghfirullaha rabbi min kulli dhambin wa atubu ilaih
options.D: La ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lah lahul mulku wa lahul hamdu

explanation: Sayyid al-Istighfar begins with 'Allahumma anta rabbi la ilaha illa anta khalaqtani wa ana abduka...' (O Allah, You are my Lord, there is no god but You, You created me and I am Your servant...). The Prophet (SAW) said that whoever recites it with conviction in the morning and dies before evening enters Paradise, and likewise for the evening.

question: What dua is specifically recommended to be recited on Laylat al-Qadr (the Night of Decree), as taught by the Prophet (SAW) to Aisha (RA)?

options.A: Allahumma innaka afuwwun tuhibbul afwa fa'fu anni
options.B: Allahumma inni as'aluka al-jannata wa a'udhu bika minan nar
options.C: Allahumma taqabbal minni innaka antas samiul alim
options.D: Rabbi hab li min ladunka rahmatan innaka antal wahhab

explanation: Aisha (RA) asked the Prophet (SAW) what dua she should make if she found Laylat al-Qadr. He replied: 'Say: Allahumma innaka afuwwun tuhibbul afwa fa'fu anni' — meaning 'O Allah, You are Pardoning, You love to pardon, so pardon me.' This concise but powerful dua focuses on seeking Allah's complete pardon on the most blessed night of the year.

source.name: Jami at-Tirmidhi; Sunan Ibn Majah
source.reference: Jami at-Tirmidhi Book 48, Hadith 3513; Sunan Ibn Majah Book 34, Hadith 3850

question: What is the dua recited between the two sajdahs (prostrations) during Salah?

options.B: Rabbi inni zalamtu nafsi zulman kathiran

explanation: Between the two prostrations (sajdahs) in Salah, the Prophet (SAW) would say 'Rabbighfirli, Rabbighfirli' — meaning 'O my Lord, forgive me, O my Lord, forgive me.' This is narrated in authentic hadith and represents one of the recommended adhkar within the prayer itself.

source.name: Sunan Ibn Majah; Sunan Abu Dawud
source.reference: Sunan Ibn Majah Book 5, Hadith 897; Sunan Abu Dawud Book 2, Hadith 874

question: What dua did Prophet Ibrahim (AS) make for his parents and the believers, as recorded in the Quran?

options.B: Rabbana taqabbal minni wa min walidayya
options.C: Rabbanaghfir li wa liwalidayya wa lilmu'minina yawma yaqumul hisab
options.D: Rabbi awzi'ni an ashkura ni'mataka allati an'amta alayya

explanation: Prophet Ibrahim (AS) made the dua: 'Rabbanaghfir li wa liwalidayya wa lilmu'minina yawma yaqumul hisab' — meaning 'Our Lord, forgive me, my parents, and the believers on the Day when the account will be established.' This dua is a model of seeking forgiveness for oneself, one's parents, and the entire Muslim community.

question: What is the dua known as 'Dua Kanzul Arsh' (the Treasure of the Throne), reported in hadith as being recited by the Prophet (SAW) during times of extreme distress?

options.A: Hasbiyallahu la ilaha illa huwa alayhi tawakkaltu wa huwa rabbul arshil azim
options.B: La ilaha illallahul halimul karim subhanallahi rabbil arshil azim
options.C: Allahumma rahmataka arju fala takilni ila nafsi tarfata ayn
options.D: Ya Hayyu ya Qayyum bi rahmatika astaghith

explanation: The dua 'La ilaha illallahul halimul karim, subhanallahi rabbil arshil azim' is known as Dua Kanzul Arsh — the Treasure of the Throne. The Prophet (SAW) said this dua is recited when a matter becomes difficult. It translates as: 'There is no god but Allah, the Forbearing, the Generous. Glory be to Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne.' It is established in both Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari; Sahih Muslim
source.reference: Sahih al-Bukhari Book 75, Hadith 6346; Sahih Muslim Book 48, Hadith 2730

question: The Prophet (SAW) taught a dua to recite in the final tashahhud before salam, seeking refuge from four things. What are those four things?

options.B: The punishment of Hellfire, the punishment of the grave, the trials of life and death, and the trial of the Dajjal

explanation: In the final tashahhud, the Prophet (SAW) instructed believers to say: 'Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min adhabil jahannam, wa min adhabil qabr, wa min fitnatil mahya wal mamat, wa min sharri fitnatil masihid dajjal' — seeking refuge from the punishment of Hell, punishment of the grave, trials of life and death, and the evil trial of the Dajjal. The Prophet (SAW) commanded this so emphatically that some companions considered it obligatory.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari; Sahih Muslim
source.reference: Sahih al-Bukhari Book 75, Hadith 6377; Sahih Muslim Book 4, Hadith 588

question: Which Quranic dua, attributed to Prophet Ayyub (AS), is considered one of the most powerful supplications for relief from hardship and illness?

options.A: Rabbi inni massaniyad durru wa anta arhamur rahimin
options.B: Allahumma la sahla illa ma ja'altahu sahla
options.C: Rabbi la tadhharni fardan wa anta khayrul waritheen
options.D: Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal wakil ni'mal mawla wa ni'man nasir

explanation: Prophet Ayyub (AS) cried out to Allah: 'Rabbi inni massaniyad durru wa anta arhamur rahimin' — meaning 'O my Lord, adversity has touched me and You are the Most Merciful of all who show mercy.' This dua, recorded in Surah Al-Anbiya, was the supplication through which Allah lifted the prolonged suffering of Ayyub (AS) and is highly recommended during times of illness and hardship.

question: What is the virtuous dua the Prophet (SAW) recommended reciting 100 times in the morning and evening, described as equivalent to freeing ten slaves, recording 100 good deeds, erasing 100 sins, and a protection from Shaytan?

options.C: La ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lahu lahul mulku wa lahul hamdu wa huwa ala kulli shayin qadir
options.D: SubhanAllahi wal hamdulillahi wa la ilaha illallahu wallahu akbar

explanation: The dhikr 'La ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lahu lahul mulku wa lahul hamdu wa huwa ala kulli shayin qadir' recited 100 times in a day carries the reward of freeing ten slaves, 100 good deeds are recorded, 100 sins are wiped away, and it serves as a protection from Shaytan throughout the day. No one on the Day of Judgement will bring anything better than this except someone who did more of it.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari; Sahih Muslim
source.reference: Sahih al-Bukhari Book 80, Hadith 6403; Sahih Muslim Book 48, Hadith 2691

question: The Prophet (SAW) described two short phrases that are 'light on the tongue, heavy on the scales, and beloved to the Most Merciful.' What are they?

options.A: La ilaha illallah and Allahu Akbar
options.B: SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi and SubhanAllahil Azim
options.D: La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah and HasbunAllah

explanation: The Prophet (SAW) said two phrases are light on the tongue, heavy on the scales of deeds, and beloved to Allah: 'SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi' (Glory be to Allah and His is the praise) and 'SubhanAllahil Azim' (Glory be to Allah, the Magnificent). This hadith is considered one of the most important reminders of how simple acts of dhikr carry immense weight with Allah.

source.name: Sahih al-Bukhari; Sahih Muslim
source.reference: Sahih al-Bukhari Book 80, Hadith 6406; Sahih Muslim Book 48, Hadith 2694

question: What is the significance of 'Ya Hayyu Ya Qayyum bi rahmatika astaghith' and in which context did the Prophet (SAW) specifically recommend it?

options.A: It is recited during the adhan and is the dua of breaking the fast
options.B: It is a dua for protection from the evil eye and is recited after Fajr
options.C: It calls upon two of Allah's greatest names and the Prophet (SAW) recommended it for times of distress, and it is connected to the greatest name of Allah (Ism al-Azam)
options.D: It is exclusively recited during the last third of the night as part of Tahajjud

explanation: 'Ya Hayyu Ya Qayyum bi rahmatika astaghith' means 'O Ever-Living, O Sustainer of existence, by Your mercy I seek relief.' Al-Hayy (the Ever-Living) and Al-Qayyum (the Self-Subsisting Sustainer of all) are considered among the greatest names of Allah (Ism al-Azam). The Prophet (SAW) heard a man supplicating using these names and said that he had called upon Allah by His greatest name, through which if asked He gives, and if called upon He responds.

source.name: Jami at-Tirmidhi; Sunan Abu Dawud
source.reference: Jami at-Tirmidhi Book 48, Hadith 3544; Sunan Abu Dawud Book 8, Hadith 1495
